tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58277,"title":"[DataBox] Docs: Fix incorrect service description and add code samples to README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` **Service directory:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` **Triggered by:** PR #57614 (commit 18df8671) by `@haiyuazhang` ## What Changed PR #57614 introduced an MPG (Management Plane Generator) migration for the Azure Data Box package, regenerating it from TypeSpec. The migration added new generated sources, custom model overrides, and set up the `1.2.0-beta.1` version. However, the README was migrated with documentation issues. ## Gaps Found - **Incorrect service description** (copy-paste error): `README.md` line 7 reads *"This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources."* — it should describe Azure Data Box resources, not Customer Insights. - **Empty Key concepts section**: The `## Key concepts` section links only to the generic Azure SDK intro page. It should describe the primary types: `DataBoxJobResource`, `DataBoxJobCollection`, and the extension methods on `ResourceGroupResource` and `SubscriptionResource`. - **No inline code samples**: The `## Examples` section contains no C# code snippets — only a link to generic management library samples. At minimum it should show how to authenticate/create a client, list Data Box jobs in a subscription, and get/create a Data Box job. - **No sample test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory or any `Sample*.cs` test file to back the README snippets via the repo's snippet-generation tooling. - **Empty CHANGELOG for 1.2.0-beta.1**: The new version section has empty `### Features Added`, `### Breaking Changes`, etc. subsections, with no notes reflecting the TypeSpec migration. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### Fix the incorrect description in README.md **File:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` **Action:** Edit Replace: ```markdown This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources. ``` With: ```markdown This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Data Box resources. ``` #### Expand the Key concepts section in README.md **File:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` **Action:** Edit — replace the existing `## Key concepts` section body with: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### DataBoxJobResource `DataBoxJobResource` represents a single Azure Data Box job and provides operations to get, update, cancel, and delete the job, as well as to retrieve shipping labels and delivery information. ### DataBoxJobCollection `DataBoxJobCollection` provides operations to list and create Data Box jobs scoped to a resource group. ### Extension methods `DataBoxExtensions` adds helper methods to: - `ResourceGroupResource` — retrieve a `DataBoxJobCollection`, get a single job by name, list available SKUs, validate inputs, and get region configuration. - `SubscriptionResource` — enumerate all Data Box jobs across the subscription, validate addresses, and get region configuration. ### Thread safety and additional concepts All client instance methods are thread-safe. See [Azure.Core concepts](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md) for client options, response access, retry policies, and logging. ``` #### Add a snippet placeholder to the Examples section **File:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` **Action:** Edit — replace the existing `## Examples` section with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Authenticate the client and get all Data Box jobs in a subscription ``` ``` ``` #### Update CHANGELOG for 1.2.0-beta.1 **File:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — populate the empty `## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Other Changes - Migrated SDK generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec (Management Plane Generator). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file: **File:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/tests/Samples/Sample1_ListDataBoxJobs.cs` **Action:** Create ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_ListDataBoxJobs : SamplesBase { [Test] [AsyncOnly] public async Task ListJobsAsync() { #region Snippet:DataBox_ListJobs ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxJobResource job in subscription.GetDataBoxJobsAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Job: {job.Data.Name}, Status: {job.Data.Status}"); } #endregion } } } ``` Add the snippet placeholder to the README (see Step 1 above) at the `## Examples` section: ``` ``` ``` ``` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will automatically populate this block with the code from the `#region Snippet:DataBox_ListJobs` / `#endregion` markers in the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current README section order is correct. The following sections need content changes per this issue: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started — Install the package | ✅ OK | None | | Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ OK | None | | Getting started — Authenticate the client | ✅ OK (links to quickstart) | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Add resource type descriptions (Step 1) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline code | Add snippet placeholder + sample test (Steps 1 & 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ OK | None | | Next steps | ✅ OK | None | | Contributing | ✅ OK | None | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databox` — verify that the README is updated with the snippet content 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databox` — regenerate public API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24557558094/agentic_workflow) · ● 554.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58277","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T09:22:43Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T09:22:43Z"}

All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_ListDataBoxJobs : SamplesBase { [Test] [AsyncOnly] public async Task ListJobsAsync() { #region Snippet:DataBox_ListJobs ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxJobResource job in subscription.GetDataBoxJobsAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Job: {job.Data.Name}, Status: {job.Data.Status}"); } #endregion } } } ``` Add the snippet placeholder to the README (see Step 1 above) at the `## Examples` section: ``` ```C# Snippet:DataBox_ListJobs ``` ``` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will automatically populate this block with the code from the `#region Snippet:DataBox_ListJobs` / `#endregion` markers in the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current README section order is correct. The following sections need content changes per this issue: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started — Install the package | ✅ OK | None | | Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ OK | None | | Getting started — Authenticate the client | ✅ OK (links to quickstart) | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Add resource type descriptions (Step 1) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline code | Add snippet placeholder + sample test (Steps 1 & 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ OK | None | | Next steps | ✅ OK | None | | Contributing | ✅ OK | None | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databox` — verify that the README is updated with the snippet content 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databox` — regenerate public API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/`

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue reports documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` README introduced by PR #57614 (TypeSpec/MPG migration), including an incorrect service description (copy-pasted from Customer Insights), missing Key concepts and Examples content, no sample test file, and an empty CHANGELOG entry.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` v1.2.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and `tests/Samples/` (missing)
**Scenarios:**
- Incorrect service description in README (says "Customer Insights" instead of "Data Box")
- Key concepts section is empty/generic — no description of `DataBoxJobResource`, `DataBoxJobCollection`, or extension methods
- Examples section has no inline C# code snippets
- No `tests/Samples/Sample*.cs` file to back snippet generation tooling
- CHANGELOG for 1.2.0-beta.1 has empty subsections with no TypeSpec migration notes

**Root ask:** Fix the README description, populate Key concepts and Examples sections with appropriate content and code snippets, create a sample test file, and update the CHANGELOG to reflect the TypeSpec migration.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This issue was automatically generated by the Update Docs workflow triggered by PR #57614 (commit 18df8671). The issue includes a detailed Implementation Guide with step-by-step instructions.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify the incorrect description at `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` line 7
2. Review the Implementation Guide in the issue body for the exact edits needed to README.md and CHANGELOG.md
3. Create `tests/Samples/Sample1_ListDataBoxJobs.cs` as described, then run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databox` to populate the README snippet placeholder
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package name `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` unambiguously belongs to the Management plane SDK category.
- **Service:** `DataBox` — The issue title, body, and package name all explicitly reference Azure Data Box.
- **Confidence:** High — Package name makes both category and service unambiguous; pattern matches quality reference issues for Mgmt + service-label pairs.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) — This is the Mgmt catch-all; no specific `%DataBox %Mgmt` entry exists in the file.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978`; no "needs-team-attention" added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from end of file upward. Specific Mgmt entries (`%ARM %Mgmt`, `%Compute - Fleet %Mgmt`, `%Datadog %Mgmt`, etc., lines 915–1086) were encountered first but none matched "DataBox". `%DataBox` entry (line 695) only requires "DataBox" but was not reached because `%Mgmt` catch-all at line 903 matched first (line 903 > line 695 in file order, so encountered earlier in bottom-to-top scan).
</details>
